claimText: India is the third largest dam-owning nation globally, following China and the USA.
evidenceExcerpt: India is the 3rd largest dam-owning nation (after China and USA)
examUse: Statement-based question on India's global standing in water infrastructure.
distractorHooks: India is the largest dam-owning nation globally.
India is second only to China in the number of large dams.

claimText: Maharashtra has the highest number of large dams among all Indian states.
evidenceExcerpt: with Maharashtra having the highest number of large dams.
examUse: State-comparison or correct-pair question regarding water infrastructure distribution.
distractorHooks: Madhya Pradesh has the highest number of large dams.
Gujarat has the highest number of large dams.

claimText: Indira Sagar Dam, located on the Narmada River in Madhya Pradesh, is India's largest reservoir by volume.
evidenceExcerpt: Indira Sagar Dam (Narmada River, MP) is India's largest reservoir by volume.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching dams to rivers/states or identifying the largest reservoir.
distractorHooks: Bhakra Nangal is India's largest reservoir by volume.
Indira Sagar Dam is located on the Godavari River.
Indira Sagar Dam is located in Maharashtra.

claimText: The Dam Safety Act, 2021 established a four-tier institutional framework comprising the NCDS, NDSA, SCDS, and SDSO.
evidenceExcerpt: Dam Safety Act, 2021 created a 4-tier institutional framework: NCDS, NDSA, SCDS, and SDSO.
examUse: Statement-based question on the institutional provisions of the Dam Safety Act, 2021.
distractorHooks: The Dam Safety Act created a 3-tier framework.
The framework is solely managed by the Central Water Commission without state-level bodies.

claimText: The Dam Rehabilitation and Improvement Project (DRIP) Phase II and III are implemented with financial assistance from the World Bank and the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB).
evidenceExcerpt: DRIP Phase II and III are implemented with financial assistance from the World Bank and AIIB.
examUse: Statement-based question on funding agencies for major national infrastructure projects.
distractorHooks: DRIP is funded by the Asian Development Bank (ADB).
DRIP is funded exclusively by the World Bank.
DRIP is funded by the International Monetary Fund (IMF).

claimText: The National Centre for Earthquake Safety of Dams is located at MNIT Jaipur.
evidenceExcerpt: National Centre for Earthquake Safety of Dams is located at MNIT Jaipur.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching specialized institutions to their locations.
distractorHooks: The National Centre for Earthquake Safety of Dams is located at IIT Roorkee.
The National Centre for Earthquake Safety of Dams is located at IIT Delhi.

claimText: The World Commission on Dams was established by the World Bank and the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) in 1998.
evidenceExcerpt: World Commission on Dams was established by the World Bank and IUCN in 1998.
examUse: Statement-based question on the origins of international environmental and infrastructure bodies.
distractorHooks: The World Commission on Dams was established by UNEP and UNESCO.
The World Commission on Dams was established solely by the World Bank.
